title: Banana Pudding
instructions: Preheat oven to 425°
Combine sugar, flour and salt in the top of a double boiler.
Mix in 1 whole egg and 3 egg yolks. (Reserve Egg whites for Meringue)
Stir in milk.
Cook, uncovered, over boiling water, stirring constantly until thickened.
Remove pudding from heat and stir in vanilla.
Spread small amount of pudding on bottom of casserole dish.
Cover with layer of Wafers then sliced bananas.
Cover with a layer of pudding.
Continue layering until you have filled your casserole dish or you have at least 3 layers.
---------- Meringue is optional ----------
Beat Reserved egg whites until stiff. (just starting to firm)
Add 1/4 cup sugar and beat until mixture it forms stiff peaks.
Put on top of pudding covering entire surface.
Bake for 5 mins or until Meringue is lightly brown.

title: Garlic Bisquits
instructions: Preheat Oven to 400°
Mix Bisquick with cheese and milk.
Use ice cream scoop to portion out the bisquits onto a greased pan
Cook until done.
Combine garlic powder, melted margarine and chives and brush onto tops of cooked bisquits

title: Pumpkin Pie
ingredients: 1 1/2 cup cooked Pumpkin or Libby Canned Pumpkin
instructions: Preheat oven to 300°
Combine all ingredients in a large bowl and mix thoroughly.
Pour into uncooked Pie Shell and bake for 1 hour

title: Frosty Ice Cream
instructions: Combine all ingredients.
Place in blender and freeze. (Wrap in cling wrap to ensure no leaks)
Once frozen blend on High until Smooth.

title: Creamy Chicken Soup w/ Potatoes and Bacon
ingredients: 1 Leek (white and green parts) halved and sliced
3/4 lb Red potatoes cut into 1/2 inch chunks
instructions: Cook bacon in large pot over medium heat until brown and crisp (about 7 - 8 mins)
Transfer to towel lined plate and reserve the pot and grease from the bacon.
Add butter and leeks to pot.
Cook, stirring occasionally, just until tender (about 3 - 4 mins)
Add garlic and fennel
Cook, stirring, until fragrant (about 1 min)
Add flour and cook, stirring (about 1 min)
Slowly whisk in milk and then stock.
Add potatoes and thyme, Salt and pepper to taste.
Bring to boil, reduce heat, and simmer until potatoes are tender (14 - 16 minutes)
Add chicken and corn.
Cook until heated through. Discard thyme. Serve topped with bacon and chivers

title: Rice Pudding
instructions: Combine sugar, eggs, milk, and butter in mixing bowl. (1 Tbsp reserved)
Add cinnamon and vanilla and mix well.
Add rice and mix well.
Pour into lightly greased 9 x 9 baking dish or pan.
Bake 350° for 35 - 40 mins
Drizzle 1 Tbsp of melted butter on top the last few minutes of baking to make top brown.

title: Pillow-Soft Rolls
ingredients: 2 pkgs(1/4 oz each) Active Dry Yeast
instructions: In large mixing bowl, combine 1 1/4 cups flour, sugar, yeast, and salt
In a sauce pan on medium heat, heat sour cream and water to 120° - 130°
Add to dry ingredients, beat until blended.
Beat in eggs until smooth
Stir to form a soft dough
Turn onto a lightly floured surface.
Knead until smooth and elastic. (about 6 - 8 mins)
Place in a greased bowl, turning once to grease the top.
Cover and refrigerate overnight.
Punch dough down and turn onto a lightly floured surface.
Roll out to 1/2 in thickness
Cut with floured 2 1/2 in biscuit cutter.
Using dull edge of knife, make an off center crease in each roll.
Fold along creased top so the small half is on top.
Press along fold edges.
Place in a greased 15 x 10 x 1 pan, allowing edges to touch.
Cover and let rise in warn place until doubled (About 25 mins)
Brush tops with butter.
Bake 375° for 12 - 15 mins or until golden brown

title: Southern Cornbread
ingredients: 2 cups White Lily Self Rising Cornmeal Mix
instructions: Preheat oven to 400° with Cast Iron skillet inside
Combine Cornmeal and sugar.
Combine milk, egg and oil
Add milk mixture all at once to cornmeal mix.
Stir until moistened (batter will be lumpy)
Pour into preheated cast iron skillet.
Bake for 20 - 25 min or until toothpick comes our clean and is golden brown.

title: Easy Bake Cheddar Biscuits
instructions: Preheat oven 450°
Mix flour, baking powder, cream of tartar, sugar and salt in medium bowl.
Cut in butter with pastry blender or two knives until mixture resembles coarse crumbles.
Stir in cheddar cheese.
Add milk until mixture forms soft dough.
Place dough on lightly floured surface
Knead 8 to 10 times or until smooth
Pat out dough into 6 in squares
Cut into 9 squares.
Place on ungreased baking sheet
Bake 10 - 12 min or until golden brown.
notes: Spread each warm biscuit with 2 1/2 tsp Philadelphia chive and onion cream cheese spread

title: Turnip Greens
ingredients: 4 small packs or 1 large bunch of Turnip Greens
instructions: Cut stems off and wash turnips in 4 sinks full of water until water is clear and no grit.
Put washed greens in large pot and cover with water. (All may not fit)
Cook Turnip greens and slowly add greens that did not fit at first (Be patient)
Once all greens are in the pot, Add fatback.
Cook for 3 - 4 hours until greens are tender, adding water and salt as needed

title: Beef Soup
ingredients: 1 medium Onion diced (Omit if you don't like onion)
instructions: In large pot add the Beef roast and cover with water and cook on medium high until tender. (About 45 - 60 mins depending on size of roast)
Remove Beef from pot, let cool and shred. (DON'T POUR OUT BROTH)
Add rice and elbow noodles to broth and cook for 30 mins or until rice and macaroni is tender.
Add crushed tomatoes, salt and pepper, and shredded beef.
Cook 5 mins and serve.

title: Pecan Encrusted Chicken
ingredients: 6 - 8 Slices of Sharp Cheddar Cheese / 1 cup Shredded Cheese
instructions: Preheat Oven to  350°
Flatten Chicken breast to about 1/8 inch thickness (Use heavy skillet and Ziplock bag)
Place 1 slice of cheese, a tsp or margarine/butter, salt and pepper on each Flatten chicken breast.
Mix Breadcrumbs and crushed pecans.
Dip Chicken in beaten egg then in breadcrumb mixture.
Sauté chicken in margarine on Medium Heat until Lightly browned on both sides (About 2 - 3 mins per side)
Place chicken in a lightly greased 10 x 15 baking dish and bake 20 - 25 mins.

title: Chicken Broccoli Casserole
ingredients: 1 can Cream of (Chicken or Mushroom) soup
3 Tbsp Mayo or Sour cream or 1 1/2 Tbsp of each. (Grandma preferred Mayo)
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°
Boil Chicken until done, then remove from bone.
Put chicken in bottom of casserole dish and add salt and pepper.
Top chicken with broccoli
Mix Soup and mayo together and add enough water to make it easy to spread over broccoli.
Spread mayo mixture over top broccoli.
Top with breadcrumbs and cheese.
Melt butter and pour over top.
Bake until cheese is melted.

title: Do Lolly
ingredients: 1 - 2 cans of Beef and Vegetable soup
instructions: Preheat oven to 375 °
Brown and drain the ground beef/chuck.
In a casserole dish layer, beef, potatoes, then onion.
Repeat until ingredients are gone or casserole dish is full.
Pour Beef and vegetable soup over top. Spread even.
Cook 30 mins or until potatoes are fork tender.
notes: (There was no cook time in the recipe 30 minutes is a guess...LOL)

title: Chicken Casserole
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°.
In large bowl mix crushed Ritz with In a 2-Qt casserole dish layer Crushed Ritz crackers (reserve 1/2 - 1 cup for topping)  pads of butter and shredded Chicken (and water chestnuts if added).
In a large bowl mix soups and sour cream and pour over layered ingredients.
Top with remaining crackers and butter.
Bake for 30 - 40 minutes until top is lightly golden.

title: Dressing
ingredients: 1 - 2 slices of toast (1/2 cup bread crumbs) (if needed)
instructions: Preheat oven to 425°
Mix all ingredients for corn bread in large bowl.
Pour cornbread mixture into 9x12 pan (should be enough for 2)
Cook Cornbread about 20 min or until done and golden brown.
Cook chicken in large pot until done and tender.
Shred chicken into large bowl.
Crumble Corn bread and bread crumbs into the large bowl.
Add onions, eggs, celery, salt, pepper and rest of ingredients.
Add Broth until the right consistency is reached.
Pour mixture in DEEP pan at least 3 to 4 inches deep and bake at 425° until lightly brown on top.

title: Forbidden Biscuits
instructions: Preheat Oven to 450°
Melt butter and cool to lukewarm. (MUST COOL TO LUKEWARM to prevent curdling)
Add lukewarm butter to sour cream and mix well (DON'T BEAT)
Stir into flow and mix until fully incorporated.
Drop teaspoon sized dollop into Tiny muffin tin.
Bake for 10 - 15 min or until golden brown.

title: Beef Stroganoff
ingredients: 2 Lbs Thin cut steak, Ground Beef/ Chuck
instructions: On Medium High heat melt margarine.
Add Onion and garlic and sauté until onions are translucent.
Add Beef and brown.
Add flour, salt, pepper, cream of mushroom and mushrooms.
Simmer on Medium to medium low for 10 mins.
Add sour cream and simmer 5 mins.
notes: Serve over rice or noodles. ( I prefer Egg Noodles)

title: Zesty Italian Dressing Chicken
ingredients: I large bottle of Italian Dressing
instructions: Place Chicken breast in large bowl.
Cover chicken with Italian dressing and let marinate for at least 1 hour.
Cook on grill or in oven until done (about 1 hour)
Brush chicken with dressing while cooking
notes: I remember this was one of Grandma's go to recipes if she didn't know what to cook.

title: Yeast Rolls
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°
Sift flour, baking powder, baking soda, salt and sugar into a large bowl.
Cut in shortening.
Soften yeast in warm water (make sure it completely dissolves)
Add warm buttermilk then add to and mix with dry ingredients.
Turn out onto a floured surface.
Knead just until elastic or smooth
Roll out and cut into biscuit size of your choosing.
Place on a greased baking pan and let rise for 1 hour in a draft free area.
Bake for 10 minutes or until lightly brown
notes: Good with Brunswich Stew (or anything else) always serve with love

title: Barbeque Sauce
instructions: Sauté Onions in margarine until tender.
Stir in remaining ingredients and simmer on medium to medium low for 5 minutes
notes:
Pour over 2 or 3 Lbs of shredded Beef that has been cooked tender.
Pour Beef and Barbeque mixture in Baking Pan.
Cover and cook for 30 minutes.

title: Brunswich Stew
ingredients: Hot Sauce and Brown sugar to taste.
instructions: Cook Hen in Large pot until done and tender.
Cook Beef Roast in another Large pot until done and tender.
Cook Butterbeans, Corn and potatoes in Beef Broth from Beef Roast until done.
Add all remaining ingredients (Use LARGE POT) and cook slowly.
Add more liquid (chicken broth) if needed
notes: Grandma always made this with her barbeque beef and yeast rolls.

title: Beef Stew
instructions: Heat oil and butter in a large pot over medium-high heat. Brown meat in two batches, then set aside.
Add diced onions to the pot and cook for 2 to 3 minutes. Add garlic and cook for another minute.
Pour in beer and beef stock. Add Worcestershire sauce, tomato paste, paprika, salt, pepper, and sugar. Return beef to the pot, cover, and simmer for 1 1/2 to 2 hours.
Add carrots and potatoes. Cover and cook for 30 more minutes. Add hot water as needed if the stew gets too thick.
Taste and adjust seasoning. Serve with crusty bread and minced parsley if desired.
notes: Recipe can be doubled or tripled if needed.

title: Pin Wheels
ingredients: 2 packages cream cheese, 8 oz each, softened
1 package ranch salad dressing mix, 1 oz
instructions: Beat cream cheese and ranch dressing mix in a small bowl. Stir in green onions and bell pepper.
Spread cream cheese mixture over each tortilla and top each tortilla with two slices of ham.
Roll tortillas tightly and wrap in plastic wrap. Refrigerate until firm, about 30 minutes.
Unwrap and cut into 3/4-inch slices.

title: Taco Rolls
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F.
Brown ground beef, drain, and return it to the pan.
Add taco seasoning and prepare according to the seasoning package directions.
Place 2 spoonfuls of taco meat on each crescent roll, add cheese, and roll up.
Bake for 15 minutes or until the crescent rolls are golden brown.
notes: Serve with salsa, sour cream, or guacamole.
